layman_headline: Crypto market stuck in fear mode — no clean entry exists right now, patience is the play.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is trading in a fearful zone with a Fear & Greed score of 29. Bitcoin is hovering near $64,000 while most altcoins are in downtrends below their key moving averages. The candidates on our watchlist are either already extended after big runs (B up 123%, PUMP up 32%, LDO up 15%) or caught in heavy selling (BONK down 26%, WLD down 14%). None present a clean risk/reward setup with a confirmed reversal signal right now.
layman_action: Wait. Don't chase coins that have already popped or try to catch falling knives. Set alerts for ETH at $1,800 — if it holds that support and bounces with rising volume, that's our next best entry.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate has significant headwinds. B and PUMP have surged 123% and 32% in 7 days respectively — chasing parabolic micro-caps with no funding rate data and bearish chart structures is high-risk gambling, not swing trading. BONK has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows — classic acceleration pattern, not a bottom. LDO has the best chart (+0.85 bias, Three White Soldiers) but RSI at 59 with only 0.38x risk/reward to resistance at $0.40 makes it a poor entry now. ETH is the most compelling watch — RSI 39 approaching oversold (our highest-confidence signal type at 80% win rate), strong institutional narrative building, but the chart bias is -0.70 with price below all MAs. We need $1,800 support to hold and a volume-confirmed bounce before committing capital. BTC dominance at 56.4% continues to suppress alts.
risk_factors: BTC dominance rising at 56.4% continues to drain capital from altcoins — any BTC weakness could cascade harder into alts
Oil bounce and AI selloff creating macro headwinds that could push risk assets lower
Fear & Greed at 29 is improving but still in fear territory — sentiment can easily reverse back toward extreme fear
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume confirmation would trigger a BUY signal
Fear & Greed index trajectory — a move above 35 would signal improving conditions for alt entries
LDO holding $0.35 and consolidating — if it pulls back to $0.33-0.34 with RSI cooling to ~50, it becomes a strong entry
market_context: Bitcoin is range-bound near $64K with oil bouncing and lingering AI selloff creating headwinds. The market is in a fear state (29/100) but slowly improving from last week's 25.9 average, suggesting we may be near a local bottom but haven't confirmed a reversal.
context_banner: Fear is improving but no high-conviction swing setup exists — disciplined patience protects capital for the next clear opportunity.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but hasn't crossed the 30 threshold yet, warranting patience.
Fear & Greed at 29 improving from weekly average of 25.9 suggests sentiment bottom may be forming, but hasn't flipped to greed which historically precedes alt rallies.
Bitcoin ETF inflows returning but described as 'peanuts' vs recent outflows — institutional conviction is weak, reducing odds of a sustained rally.
ETH derivatives reset narrative and Grayscale ETH staking comparison could build a catalyst for ETH if institutional positioning shifts.
LDO shows Three White Soldiers (+0.85 bias) — strongest chart among candidates — but poor risk/reward at current levels (0.38x) makes chasing inadvisable.
